title: Toothbrush heads comprising a two-component-cleaning element and method for producing the same
abstract: Brush heads, in particular toothbrush heads comprising bristle tuft cleaning elements and two-component-(2K)-cleaning elements can be produced by injection molding. In order to achieve high design flexibility regarding the arrangement of the 2K-cleaning elements and regarding the material choice, the 2K-cleaning elements may be manufactured separately or in an upstream molding step. Then the 2K-cleaning elements together with the bristle tuft(s) may be over-molded with plastic material in order to form the brush head or a part thereof.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What we claim is: 1. A method for producing a brush head (10), in particular a toothbrush head, or a part thereof comprising the steps of -providing a cleaning element carrier (30) comprising cavities (31,32), wherein the cleaning element carrier (30) represents a first half of a mold; -providing at least one two-component-(2K)-cleaning element (20) wherein the 2K- cleaning element comprises a first component (21) and a second component (22), wherein the second component (22) comprise at least one undercut; -inserting the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) into the cleaning element carrier (30), wherein the first component (21) of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and a first part (27) of the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are covered by the cleaning element carrier (30); -forming a mold cavity (36) by combining the cleaning element carrier (30) with at least a second mold half (34), wherein the second component (22) of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) including the at least one undercut and a second part (28) of the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are located inside the mold cavity (36); -injecting plastic material into the mold cavity (36), wherein the second component (22) of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) including the at least one undercut and the second part(s) of the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are over-molded by the injected plastic material and wherein the brush head (10) or the part thereof is formed thereby, and wherein the 2K-cleaning element (20) is mounted physically into the brush head (10) by the at least one undercut. 2. The method according to the preceding claim, wherein the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are inserted into the cleaning element carrier (30) before or after the at least one 2K- cleaning element (20). 3. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) consists of a plurality of individual filament fibers (25), wherein the plurality of filament fibers (25) are fused to form a filament tuft (26) when they are located in the cleaning element carrier (30). 4. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims comprising the step of providing an insert (12) to the mold cavity (36), wherein a part of the insert (12) is located inside the mold cavity (36) and over-molded by the injected plastic material. 5. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein the step of providing the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) comprises inserting the first component (21) into the cleaning element carrier (30) and over-molding the first component with the material of the second component (22) to form the 2K-cleaning element (20). 6. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein the first (21) and second (22) component of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) comprise two different hard plastic materials, two different soft plastic materials, or a hard plastic material and a soft plastic material, preferably wherein the first (21) and second (22) component of the 2K-cleaning element (20) consists of a hard plastic material and an elastomeric cleaning element, of a hard or soft plastic material and an indicator element, of a hard or soft plastic material and a bundle of bristles, of a hard or soft plastic material and a bundle of super-thin tapered bristles, of a hard or soft plastic material and a chemical releasing element or a combination thereof. 7. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one 2K- cleaning element (20) further comprises a third (23) and a fourth (24) component, wherein all components of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) consists of different materials, preferably different plastic materials. 8. The method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein more than one cleaning element carrier (30) are provided in parallel and more than one brush heads (10) or parts thereof are formed in parallel. 9. A manual toothbrush (40) or a part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush comprising a brush head (10) providing at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) obtainable by a method according to anyone of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are embedded into the plastic material of the brush head (10) by over- molding, wherein the plastic material of the brush head (10) is different to the materials of 

 the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26), and wherein the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and the brush head (10) or the part of a replacement brush head (42) are physically connected by at least one undercut. 10. The manual toothbrush (40) or the part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush according to claim 9, wherein the two components (21, 22) of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) are selected from two different hard plastic materials, from two different soft plastic materials, or from a hard plastic material and a soft plastic material, preferably wherein the 2K-cleaning element (20) consists of a hard plastic material and an elastomeric cleaning element, of a hard or soft plastic material and an indicator element, of a hard or soft plastic material and a bundle of bristles, of a hard or soft plastic material and a bundle of super-thin tapered bristles, of a hard or soft plastic material and a chemical releasing element or a combination thereof. 11. The manual toothbrush (40) or the part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush, according to claim 10, wherein the elastomeric cleaning element is a nub, a pin, a fin, a wall, a bar, a gutter, a curve, a circle, a textured element, a polishing element, a tongue cleaning element or a combination thereof. 12. The manual toothbrush (40) or the part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush according to anyone of claims 9 to 11, wherein the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) further comprises a third (23) and a fourth (24) component, wherein all components of the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) consists of different materials, preferably different plastic materials. 13. The part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush according to anyone of claims 9 to 12 wherein the brush head (10) further comprises an insert (12), wherein the insert (12) is embedded into the brush head (10) by over-molding together with the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) and the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) and wherein the insert (12) and the at least one 2K-cleaning element (20) as well as the one or more bristle tuft(s) (26) are located at opposite sides of the brush head (10). 14. The part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush according to claim 13, wherein the insert (12) is a drive part, a coupling element connectable to a driving 

 shaft or a drive part, a part of a bearing, a bearing or a bearing shaft, a carrier spindle or a security anchor or a combination thereof. The part of a replacement brush head (42) for an electric toothbrush according to claims 13 or 14, wherein the insert (12) consists of a plastic material having a higher Shore hardness than the material of the brush head (10), a metal, a metal alloy or a combination thereof.
